attacking_style: Sprinter who launches from the front after climbs, capitalizing on positioning chaos and surviving moderately hard terrain that eliminates pure sprinters.
team_tactics: Groupama-FDJ controls pace on long, shallow climbs (20km at 4%) to thin the sprint field, positions Demar at front for finale after regrouping post-climb.
terrain_preference: Rolling stages with long, shallow gradients (4%) that drop pure sprinters but allow him to survive and contest reduced bunch sprints.
vulnerability: Reliant on opportunistic wins when lead-out trains fail; dropped on climbs requiring team support to regain contact before finales.
key_patterns: Won both stages after chaos disrupted organized lead-out trains in final kilometer.
Gets dropped on climbs but rejoins peloton, then immediately deploys team tactically.
Team paces climbs specifically to eliminate pure sprinters like Cavendish and Ewan.
Executes front-running sprints without traditional lead-out after regaining contact post-climb.
form_trajectory: positive: back-to-back Giro stage wins showing peak form, surviving climbs and capitalizing on chaos
